mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack triggers earnings recession fears that force equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts signal collapsing consumer demand that directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during periods of elevated consumer price inflation indicates capacity constraints that compress profit margins and generate negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during CPIAUCSL expansions above the 80th percentile yields positive 20-day returns in over 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate levels above 4.0 percent impose severe financing constraints on highly leveraged firms and trigger rapid equity deleveraging that produces negative returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 58% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly expands corporate earnings capacity and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in out-of-sample regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during active monetary tightening cycles signal forced economic contraction that accelerates equity sell-offs over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations.
